All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

similar_previous.semantic_text: How the contest is shaping up two weeks ahead of crucial Makerfield by-election What stood out from the Question Time exchanges between candidates in the Makerfield by-election? What stood out from the Question Time exchanges between candidates in the Makerfield by-election? Учитывать, что сегодня это влияет на повестку городской политики. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

evidence_texts: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

evidence_texts: The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

evidence_texts: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." evidence_texts: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

And it’s not just Reform candidate Rob Kenyon’s old tweets about Carol Vorderman On a former scrapyard in the mining village of Bickershaw sits 25,000 tonnes of stinking toxic waste. This video can not be played The Green Party's Sarah Wakefield answered questions on BBC Radio Manchester The Green Party can find "better solutions for the people of Makerfield", its candidate for the by-election in the constituency has said Sarah Wakefield told BBC Radio Manchester she was "running because we are at a time in politics when there's a lot of anger and a lot of frustration" "What we need is hope and joy and better solutions for the people of Makerfield and for the country," she added Wakefield was speaking as part of a series of interviews with candidates from the major parties standing in the poll, which will take place on 18 June

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

Makerfield by-election 2026 Andy Burnham says 'give it a rest' as CCTV shows 'someone up to no good' A man with a van is shown to rip down signs in support of Mr Burnham News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 19:34, 07 Jun 2026 Updated Andy Burnham has hit out after CCTV appeared to show a man pulling down campaign signs in the Makerfield constituency over the weekend The Greater Manchester Mayor, who is Labour's candidate standing in the upcoming by-election, shared the video on his campaign social media page he claimed depicted a man 'up to no good' "Whoever this is, we’d appreciate your help on the 19th of June as we’ll have about 650 stakes to take down, but could you give it a rest for now," the post penned. The video was captioned: "Someone's up to no good in Makerfield." The short clip showed a white van pulling up on a residential street before a man is seen viole

Makerfield by-election 2026 We spent Saturday afternoon in a Makerfield pub talking politics 'It's a big deal. We're basically voting for a new Prime Minister' View 4 Images Alan Stephens in the beer garden of The Hawk. The Makerfield by-election will News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 17:06, 07 Jun 2026 Updated 07:40, 08 Jun 2026 Alan Stephens is in good spirits. He's sat in the beer garden of his local after a Saturday morning bike ride with his son The sun's out, it's the day before his 45th birthday and he's just ordered his first pint. But it's another first that Alan is keen to talk about On June 18 when Makerfield goes to the polls for a by-election that could have a big say in the future direction of the UK, Alan says he will vote for the very first time in his life. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

evidence_texts: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

What stood out from the Question Time exchanges between candidates in the Makerfield by-election? Учитывать, что сегодня это влияет на повестку городской политики. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race

No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber and local councillor

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

This video can not be played The Green Party's Sarah Wakefield answered questions on BBC Radio Manchester The Green Party can find "better solutions for the people of Makerfield", its candidate for the by-election in the constituency has said Sarah Wakefield told BBC Radio Manchester she was "running because we are at a time in politics when there's a lot of anger and a lot of frustration" "What we need is hope and joy and better solutions for the people of Makerfield and for the country," she added Wakefield was speaking as part of a series of interviews with candidates from the major parties standing in the poll, which will take place on 18 June

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

similar_previous.semantic_text: How the contest is shaping up two weeks ahead of crucial Makerfield by-election What stood out from the Question Time exchanges between candidates in the Makerfield by-election? What stood out from the Question Time exchanges between candidates in the Makerfield by-election? Учитывать, что сегодня это влияет на повестку городской политики. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

evidence_packet.evidence_text: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random and Andy Burnham doesn't suffer from a deficit of ambition In fact his very mention of the prospect of a contest goaded Downing Street into restating the prime minister's position, with a No10 spokesman pointing out that "the Labour Party has a process for challenging a leader and it has not been triggered. evidence_texts: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

This video can not be played The Green Party's Sarah Wakefield answered questions on BBC Radio Manchester The Green Party can find "better solutions for the people of Makerfield", its candidate for the by-election in the constituency has said Sarah Wakefield told BBC Radio Manchester she was "running because we are at a time in politics when there's a lot of anger and a lot of frustration" "What we need is hope and joy and better solutions for the people of Makerfield and for the country," she added Wakefield was speaking as part of a series of interviews with candidates from the major parties standing in the poll, which will take place on 18 June

And it’s not just Reform candidate Rob Kenyon’s old tweets about Carol Vorderman On a former scrapyard in the mining village of Bickershaw sits 25,000 tonnes of stinking toxic waste. As Chris Gee reports , the ‘ocean of rubbish’ first came to light last year and later a week-long fire saw dangerous fumes released into the atmosphere and forced nearby schools A criminal investigation is underway to find out who was responsible, with a 58-year-old man currently on bail after being arrested in Birmingham. evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

He's sat in the beer garden of his local after a Saturday morning bike ride with his son The sun's out, it's the day before his 45th birthday and he's just ordered his first pint. But it's another first that Alan is keen to talk about On June 18 when Makerfield goes to the polls for a by-election that could have a big say in the future direction of the UK, Alan says he will vote for the very first time in his life. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

What stood out from the Question Time exchanges between candidates in the Makerfield by-election? Учитывать, что сегодня это влияет на повестку городской политики. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

evidence_texts: Share Save Add as preferred on Google Georgie Docker North West REU A total of 14 hopefuls will now run in the upcoming poll in Makerfield A further five candidates have been announced ahead of the Makerfield by-election in Greater Manchester Standing as independents are John Dyer, Paul Gould and Robert Pownall - with Ed Gemmell announced as the Climate Party's candidate and Count Binface running for the Count Binface Party There are now a total of 14 hopefuls set to contest the parliamentary seat on 18 June The by-election was called after Labour's Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

We're basically voting for a new Prime Minister' View 4 Images Alan Stephens in the beer garden of The Hawk. The Makerfield by-election will News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 17:06, 07 Jun 2026 Updated 07:40, 08 Jun 2026 Alan Stephens is in good spirits. He's sat in the beer garden of his local after a Saturday morning bike ride with his son The sun's out, it's the day before his 45th birthday and he's just ordered his first pint. But it's another first that Alan is keen to talk about On June 18 when Makerfield goes to the polls for a by-election that could have a big say in the future direction of the UK, Alan says he will vote for the very first time in his life. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

evidence_texts: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

evidence_texts: The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Green Party Sarah Wakefield is standing for the Green Party The Green Party has announced that Sarah Wakefield will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Wakefield, a councillor on Manchester City Council, was chosen after the Greens' first candidate, Chris Kennedy, withdrew from the race The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down Wakefield said it was "vital in a democracy that voters are given a choice of who they want to vote for" The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Reuters People living in the Makerfield constituency in Greater Manchester will soon have the opportunity to elect their new MP Fourteen candidates will contest the Makerfield by-election on Thursday 18 June All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017

What stood out from the Question Time exchanges between candidates in the Makerfield by-election? Учитывать, что сегодня это влияет на повестку городской политики. It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

All of the candidates standing in the Greater Manchester constituency are listed below in alphabetical order, by surname The Liberal Democrats have announced Jake Austin as their candidate The Stockport councillor was born in the constituency and contested the Greater Manchester mayoral election in 2024 The Labour Party has officially confirmed Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham as its candidate Burnham, a former Labour government minister, has been the region's metropolitan mayor since 2017 The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after then-Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said was to allow Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid The vote in Makerfield is expected to be a race between Burnham and Kenyon, a plumber a

The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

evidence_texts: It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random

The Greater Manchester mayor made clear his ambitions as he appeared alongside four other candidates during a BBC Question Time special in the constituency Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon said there was a need for a "big move away from career politicians" and warned against using Makerfield as a "stepping stone" Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defied calls from some Labour MPs to step down after poor election results last month. No formal challenge has been launched against him A No 10 spokesperson said: "The prime minister will not walk away from the mandate he was given just two years ago to build a stronger, fairer Britain." The by-election was called after Labour MP Josh Simons stepped down, a move he said he had undertaken to allow his party colleague Andy Burnham to run for Parliament and potentially mount a leadership bid Speaking to BBC Radio Manchester , Robert Kenyon said if he won, he becomes an MP, but if he lost, he had made his point as "a working class lad who has given two fingers to the establishment" The former Army reservist also dismissed comments he made in relation to TV presenter Carol Vorderman as "squaddie humour" Vorderman demanded an apology after Kenyon responded to a sexually graphic post about the TV presenter with a thumbs up and laughing emoji and a comment saying "He's only saying what we're all thinking" Kenyon admitted the comment made about Vorderman was "disgusting" but described it as a "crass joke", adding: "The person I made it to was ex-military, so it was just of what w

Share Save Add as preferred on Google Libertarian Party Dan Clarke is standing for the Libertarian Party The Libertarian Party has announced that Dan Clarke will be its candidate in the Makerfield by-election Clarke, who is the party's co-ordinator for the North-West, stood in the Gorton and Denton by-election, also in Greater Manchester, in February He said voters in Makerfield "deserve the option to support a candidate willing to challenge the government, stand up for local constituents, and put personal freedom at the heart of politics" The poll, which will take place on 18 June, comes after Labour MP Josh Simons announced he was standing down The other confirmed candidates in the Makerfield by-election are Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . It is worth looking at his wording carefully: "I think Wes Streeting seems to have launched a leadership contest, so if that is running, I would seek to join it. But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time

But I'd have to persuade members of the Parliamentary Labour Party to do the same." Clearly Burnham is desperate not to be seen to be presumptuous, at any potential stage in this process – not least to the people of the Makerfield constituency who he needs to win over in the next couple of weeks Streeting, the former health secretary, has not formally launched a contest either, but Burnham is suggesting he would join an existing contest rather than trigger one himself This is a subtle distinction, but a distinction Burnham's allies do point to – to again seek to emphasise he is not trigger happy, he is not seeking to get ahead of himself But let's be frank: Fiona Bruce and Question Time weren't in Ashton-in-Makerfield at random
